definition: An 8-step transmission chain connects geopolitical events to financial market impacts, with historical calibration across four major episodes (1973, 1979, 1990, 2022). Recent case studies (WTI negative, LME nickel, European gas margin calls) demonstrate financial amplification mechanisms.
evidence: Financial amplification case studies: WTI negative, LME nickel, €1.5T European gas margin calls
Systematic framework for geopolitical-to-financial risk assessment
open_questions: Are amplification channels in the current configuration different from 1970s or comparable in scale?
How does elevated sovereign debt interact with commodity shocks in this transmission chain?
